issues.description: Badge contrast overrides break when OS=light + data-theme="dark". The media query `@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { :root .badge-converged { color: var(--success); } }` has specificity (0,1,1), which beats the base rule `.badge-converged { color: #3A9A65; }` at (0,1,0). When a user on a light-mode OS forces dark theme via the toggle, the media query still fires (it's OS-based, not data-theme-based), overriding the dark badge color #3A9A65 back to var(--success) = #2D7A4F. This is the exact insufficient-contrast value (~3.05:1 against dark --surface #1A1918) that the #3A9A65 override was introduced to fix. Same issue applies to .badge-failed reverting from #E0564C to var(--error) = #C4392D.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit `[data-theme="dark"] .badge-converged, [data-theme="dark"] .badge-hardened, [data-theme="dark"] .badge-shipped { color: #3A9A65; }` and `[data-theme="dark"] .badge-failed { color: #E0564C; }` rules after the media query block. These have specificity (0,2,0) and will correctly win over the media query's (0,1,1) in the OS-light + forced-dark scenario.

issues.description: The CSS comment claims '#C4392D ≈4.5:1 vs #F0EFED (light --surface)' for the --error badge in light mode. The actual contrast ratio of #C4392D against #F0EFED is approximately 4.0:1 — below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA threshold for normal text. The spec FR-3c requires badge text to 'pass 4.5:1 against both --surface variants'. This means the light-mode badge-failed fallback to var(--error) may not meet the spec requirement.
issues.suggestion: Verify the exact contrast ratio of #C4392D against #F0EFED with a calculator. If it fails 4.5:1, use a lighter error color for light-mode badges (e.g., #B4281E from helm's light theme, or a value that passes). Update the comment with the verified ratio.

issues.description: The CSS comment claims '--text-secondary: #5C5A57 is ~6.6:1 vs #FAFAF8'. The actual contrast ratio of #5C5A57 against #FAFAF8 is approximately 5.0:1, not 6.6:1. While it still passes WCAG AA 4.5:1, the documented ratio is incorrect and misleading. The spec says 'the implementor must verify and pick the value that passes 4.5:1 while remaining readable. Document the measured ratio.'
issues.suggestion: Correct the comment to the actual measured ratio (~5.0:1). Same applies to --text-tertiary on line 56 which copies the same incorrect claim.

issues.description: Light-mode --text-secondary and --text-tertiary are both #5C5A57, eliminating any visual hierarchy between secondary and tertiary text in light mode. The spec FR-1f acknowledges this is pragmatic and explicitly directs bumping to #5C5A57, so this is spec-compliant but worth noting as a known limitation.
issues.suggestion: No code change needed — document this as a known limitation. A follow-up could find an intermediate value that passes 4.5:1 while being visually distinct from --text-secondary.

issues.description: Server-rendered HTML hardcodes aria-checked="true" on System and "false" on Dark/Light. If the user previously selected Dark or Light (stored in localStorage), the ARIA state is incorrect until JS runs. The menu starts hidden and initTheme() runs on DOMContentLoaded, so this is not user-visible, but a screen reader accessing the DOM before JS initializes would see stale state.
issues.suggestion: Acceptable for v0.6.1 given the menu is hidden until JS runs. No change needed.
